page: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, the instance features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. Hardware-assisted virtualization is provided via Microsoft Hyper-V. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average range. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations; the stress-ng multi-core CPU score and Geekbench multi-core score are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including read-only and write large block tests, also register in the weak tier, though Passmark memory performance remains average. Cryptographic and compression tests, such as gzip single-threaded compression, demonstrate average throughput, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor.

From a resource density perspective, the E8-4s_v4 offers a high memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. The primary tradeoff is the lower multi-core compute and memory bandwidth performance relative to the large memory capacity. This profile is suitable for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers with moderate throughput requirements, and enterprise applications requiring a large memory footprint but limited concurrent CPU utilization.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ESv4 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, it provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, contrasted by weak multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, this server offers high memory density relative to its compute allocation, presenting a distinct tradeoff between memory capacity and parallel processing power. It is suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU demands, such as smaller database deployments, caching systems, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering high memory density for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine delivering high memory density relative to compute resources for memory-bound enterprise applications.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local storage or integrated GPUs, with support for Premium Storage
Average single-core performance paired with weak multi-core benchmark results
Suited for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads with moderate CPU needs
